headline: PROGRESS IN GENERATING UNIQUE DIGITAL FARMER IDS
fullText:
Ministry of Agriculture &

PROGRESS IN GENERATING UNIQUE DIGITAL FARMER IDS

24 JUL 2026 5:57PM by PIB Delhi

As of 20th July, 2026, more than 10.18 Crore Farmer IDs have been created to streamline the direct transfer of subsidies and technical advisories. The State-wise progress of Farmer IDs generated is placed at Annexure-I. States are using Farmer IDs to ease the lives of farmers. It enables the integration of Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) schemes such as registrations under Pradhan Mantri Kisan Samman Nidhi Yojna (PM Kisan), Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojna (PMFBY), Minimum Support Price (MSP) based procurement, access to credit delivery, input distribution, and disaster relief.

The integration of satellite imagery has optimized and streamlined the Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana (PMFBY) operations through the following initiatives:

Yield Estimation System based on Technology (YES-TECH): Satellite Remote sensing data, coupled with various crop yield estimation models, is leveraged to generate technology based crop yield at Insurance unit level. This significantly supplements Crop Cutting Experiments (CCEs) yield data, addressing localized yield anomalies with high accuracy.

Smart Sampling for Crop Cutting Experiments (CCEs): Instead of random field selections, satellite- derived vegetation indices identify variations in crop health and this allows optimization of the planning of CCEs, eliminating manual selection biases and accelerating data collection.

Dispute Resolution: Remote sensing provides an objective assessment of crop conditions across seasons using time series satellite images. This data serves as an effective tool to resolve area discrepancy and yield disputes between State Governments and Insurance Companies in short turn-around time, eliminating delays in claim settlements.

Rapid Crop Damage & Inundation Assessment: In the event of localized natural calamities like floods, droughts, or severe weather anomalies, multi-temporal satellite imagery enables rapid mapping of the affected area. Through the automated workflows of the Krishi Decision Support System (DSS), the turnaround time for assessment of food inundation and drought monitoring has significantly reduced.

The various initiatives have been taken to train and equip for utilizing agricultural drones, such as use of drones are promoted under Sub-Mission on Agriculture Mechanization (SMAM) by providing assistance up to 50% for agriculture graduates, Small and Marginal farmers of the North Eastern States for procuring kisan drones. For other farmers, financial assistance for the purchase of Kisan Drones is provided at 40% of the cost. Financial assistance of 40% of the cost is also provided for establishing Custom Hiring Centres of Kisan Drones. A total of 2122 Drones have been distributed/approved under SMAM scheme.

The Farm Machinery Training & Testing Institutes (FMTTIs), which are subordinate offices of the Department of Agriculture & Farmers’ Welfare located at Budni (Madhya Pradesh), Hisar (Haryana), and Biswanath Chariali (Assam), are DGCA-approved RPTOs conducting training on drones. To date, these institutes have trained 155 drone pilots. The expenditure towards these training programmes is met by the Institutes from their annual budget.

The comprehensive 15-day training programme on the operation of agricultural drones, including obtaining a Drone Pilot Licence, is an integral component of the drone supply package under the Namo Drone Didi Scheme. As the scheme is exclusively for the identified Women Self Help Groups (SHGs), the members of each selected SHG are trained as drone pilots. These trainings are conducted in Directorate General of Civil Aviation (DGCA) approved Remote Pilot Training Organization (RPTOs), which are presently 274 in number across the country. Under this scheme, 1094 drones have been provided to the selected SHGs.

headline: Two-day Orientation Programme for Newly Elected and Nominated Members of Rajya Sabha begins tomorrow
fullText:
Rajya Sabha Secretariat

Two-day Orientation Programme for Newly Elected and Nominated Members of Rajya Sabha begins tomorrow

Hon’ble Chairman, Rajya Sabha, Shri C.P. Radhakrishnan to inaugurate the Orientation Programme

Senior Rajya Sabha Members and Officers of the Secretariat to guide newly elected and nominated Members

24 JUL 2026 5:54PM by PIB Delhi

Experienced sitting Members of Rajya Sabha and senior officers of the Secretariat will guide the newly elected and nominated Members on the topics related to the procedures and functioning of Parliament, particularly Rajya Sabha, during a two-day Orientation Programme to be inaugurated tomorrow by Hon'ble Chairman, Rajya Sabha, Shri C. P. Radhakrishnan.

The Rajya Sabha Secretariat is organising a two-day interactive programme on 25th and 26th July, 2026 for the benefit of newly elected and nominated Members of Rajya Sabha. The two-day Orientation Programme will begin with a Welcome Address by Hon'ble Deputy Chairman, Rajya Sabha, Shri Harivansh.

Hon'ble Chairman, Rajya Sabha, Shri C.P. Radhakrishnan will deliver ‘Keynote address outlining the vision and responsibilities of the Members’; Hon’ble Deputy Chairman, Shri Harivansh will speak on ‘The functions and roles of key Parliamentary Committees’; Shri Ashwini Vaishnaw, M/o Railways; Information & Broadcasting; and Electronics & Information Technology will speak on ‘Hands-on IT workshop: Understanding the impact of AI, Blockchain, and Data Privacy laws on future legislation and public service delivery’ during the two-day programme. Other senior Members of Parliament viz. Dr. John Brittas, Shri Ghanshyam Tiwari, Shri Sujeet Kumar and Shri Pramod Tiwari are also likely to conduct interactive sessions of legislative relevance during the programme.

Shri P.C. Mody, Secretary-General, Rajya Sabha will speak on ‘Introduction to the structure, functions, and services provided by the Secretariat and ‘Formal expression of gratitude to the dignitaries and attendees’. Other senior officers of Rajya Sabha Secretariat will speak on the topics of Parliamentary importance during the two-day programme. Dr. K.S. Somashekhar, Secretary will give the final Vote of Thanks marking the end of the two-day programme.

headline: PROGRESS UNDER DDU-GKY
fullText:
Ministry of Rural Development

PROGRESS UNDER DDU-GKY

24 JUL 2026 5:47PM by PIB Delhi

DDU-GKY is one of the cluster of initiatives under Deen Dayal Antyodaya Yojana - National Rural Livelihoods Mission (DAY-NRLM) aimed at livelihood diversification and poverty reduction. DDU-GKY aims to skill rural youth and promote sustainable livelihoods for rural youth through placement linked skilling programs.

Under DDU-GKY, efforts are made to align the aspirations of rural youth with the evolving skill requirements of the labour market. States/UTs are provided budgetary support to undertake skill gap studies to identify sectoral opportunities and employment potential in their regions. Based on these studies, States/UTs and Project Implementing Agencies (PIAs) are encouraged to propose training programmes in emerging and high-growth sectors aligned with local, domestic and global job markets. While submitting project proposals, PIAs are required to provide justification and demand assessment for the sectors proposed, ensuring that training under DDU-GKY remains demand-driven and industry-relevant.

So far under DDUGKY, a total of 18.45 lakh candidates has been trained and 12.35 lakh have been placed till June 2026.

The sectors that have been identified for the training and placement of rural youth under the said scheme are given as Annexure I.

Funds are released to Project Implementing Agencies (PIAs), subject to achieving the placement target. PIAs are required to ensure placement of at least 70% of trained candidates, with at least 50% placed in wage employment. Post Placement Support (PPS) is provided to eligible candidates to facilitate retention for a period of six months and tracking for up to twelve months.

The Budget Estimate (BE) for the FY 2026-27 is ₹ 750.00 crore as Central Share.

This information was given by Minister of State for the Rural Development, Shri Kamlesh Paswan in a written reply in Rajya Sabha today.

headline: STATUS OF IMPLEMENTATION OF PMGSY
fullText:
Ministry of Rural Development

STATUS OF IMPLEMENTATION OF PMGSY

24 JUL 2026 5:45PM by PIB Delhi

Under the various ongoing verticals of the Pradhan Mantri Gram Sadak Yojana (PMGSY), a total of 2,905 roads covering 15,647.78 km and 1,042 bridges were completed across the country during the financial year 2025–26. In addition, 93 eligible habitations were provided with all-weather road connectivity.

Pradhan Mantri Gram Sadak Yojana-I (PMGSY-I) was launched as a one-time special intervention to provide rural connectivity, by way of a single all-weather road, to the eligible unconnected habitations in the core network for uplifting the socio-economic condition of the rural population. Under this scheme 1,62,876 habitations were provided connectivity. This scheme was closed on 31.03.2025 and no new further sanction was issued. However, timeline extension was given up to 31.03.2027 to complete any of the balance works and payments. Subsequently, new intervention/verticals namely PMGSY-II for up-gradation of 50,000 Km of the existing rural roads and PMGSY-III for consolidation of 1, 25,000 Km Through Routes and Major Rural Links connecting habitations, inter-alia, to Gramin Agricultural Markets (GrAMs), Higher Secondary Schools and Hospitals, were added under the ambit of PMGSY. PMGSY II and III are schemes to provide all weather connectivity to habitations and connectivity to government institutions. Since September 2024, PMGSY-IV has been launched with an objective to provide connectivity to 25000 unconnected habitations by the year 2029.

State/UT wise details may be accessed at home page of programme website https://pmgsy.dord.gov.in >habitations coverage.

(b) & (c): The implementation plan for PMGSY during the Financial Year 2026–27 focuses on the timely completion of ongoing works and up-gradation of rural roads and strengthening the maintenance of the already constructed roads. The key components of the implementation plan are as follows:

(i) Expeditious completion of ongoing works sanctioned under the various verticals of PMGSY (except PMGSY II which has been closed since 31.03.2026).

(ii) Sanction of rural roads projects under PMGSY-IV, in accordance with the approved framework and scheme guidelines, to provide all weather connectivity to unconnected habitations.

(iii) Maintenance of PMGSY roads by the respective State Governments/UT Administrations in accordance with the programme guidelines.

(iv) Strengthening digital maintenance management through the Electronic Maintenance of Rural Roads (eMARG) platform for planning, inspections, performance-based payments and monitoring of maintenance works.

(v) Quality assurance through the three-tier quality monitoring mechanism comprising Programme Implementation Units (PIUs), State Quality Monitors (SQMs) and National Quality Monitors (NQMs), and supplemented by geo-tagged inspections.

(vi) Regular monitoring and capacity building through review meetings, Regional Review Meetings (RRMs), field inspections and advisories to ensure timely implementation, quality construction and sustainable maintenance of rural road assets.

These measures are aimed at achieving the annual physical targets for FY 2026–27 while ensuring durable, high-quality rural road infrastructure and improved connectivity across the country. Accordingly, a target has been fixed for the completion of 26,474 km of rural roads, 1,375 bridges and connectivity to 2,400 eligible habitations, during FY 2026–27, under the various verticals of PMGSY, including PMGSY-I, PMGSY-III, Road Connectivity Project for Left Wing Extremism Affected Areas (RCPLWEA), PM-JANMAN and PMGSY-IV (given at Annexure-I).

Under PMGSY, special emphasis is accorded to improving rural road connectivity in Aspirational Districts, tribal areas, Left Wing Extremism (LWE)-affected areas and other remote and difficult regions, in accordance with the scheme guidelines and approved framework. Accordingly, the Government has adopted the following mechanisms to prioritize road connectivity in such areas:

(i) Priority to eligible unconnected habitations located in Aspirational Districts, tribal and remote areas while preparing proposals under the relevant verticals of PMGSY, subject to eligibility under the scheme by identifying such projects under Comprehensive New Connectivity Priority List (CNCPL). The sanctions and implementation of projects are done as per the CNCPL list.

(ii) Dedicated interventions such as the Road Connectivity Project for Left Wing Extremism Affected Areas (RCPLWEA) to improve connectivity in LWE-affected districts.

(iii) Population norms relaxation are also provided in these areas 250+ in North-Eastern States, Hill States/UTs & special category areas and 100+ in LWE affected districts, which have become eligible as per 2011 census.

(iv) PM-JANMAN, under which road connectivity is being provided to eligible Particularly Vulnerable Tribal Group (PVTG) habitations with a population of 100+, to improve access to essential services and socio-economic opportunities.

These measures are intended to promote balanced regional development and improve access to education, healthcare, markets and other essential services in underserved rural areas.

The fund-sharing pattern of PMGSY is in the ratio of 60:40 between the Centre and States for all States and UTs with Legislature except 8 North-East (NE) States and 2 Himalayan States and 1 UT (Himachal Pradesh, Uttarakhand and Jammu & Kashmir). It is 90:10 between the Centre and States for 8 NE States including Sikkim and 2 Himalayan States and 1 UT (Himachal Pradesh, Uttarakhand and Jammu & Kashmir) and 100% central share for UTs without legislature (Andaman & Nicobar and Ladakh). The funds are released on the basis of works in hand, the progress achieved in the project and after due submission of necessary documents. The fund releases under PMGSY is through the SNA SPARSH system. The details of budget allocation and sanction for the FY 2026-27 is given in Annexure-II.

This information was given by Minister of State for the Rural Development, Shri Kamlesh Paswan in a written reply in Rajya Sabha today.

headline: SHGS FORMED UNDER DAY-NRLM
fullText:
Ministry of Rural Development

SHGS FORMED UNDER DAY-NRLM

24 JUL 2026 5:43PM by PIB Delhi

Under Deendayal Antyodaya Yojana–National Rural Livelihoods Mission (DAY-NRLM), 92 lakh Self Help Groups (SHGs) have been formed across the country, covering 10.05 crore rural women.

Under DAY-NRLM, the Ministry is promoting financial inclusion of women Self Help Group (SHG) members through financial literacy, enhanced access to institutional credit, and promotion of micro-enterprises.

To deepen financial inclusion in rural areas, particularly among women SHG members, DAY-NRLM has launched a comprehensive Financial Literacy Programme in partnership with Rural Self Employment Training Institutes (RSETIs) and banks. Financial Literacy Community Resource Persons conduct village-level financial literacy camps covering modules on Financial Planning, Savings, Credit, Insurance, Pension, Enterprise Financing, and Business Correspondence. During the Financial Year 2025–26, more than 3.81 crore women SHG members were imparted financial literacy training on these modules.

Further, all Public Sector Banks and one private sector bank have designed their own dedicated credit products for individual women SHG members under DAY-NRLM to facilitate the Lakhpati Didi initiative. These credit products enable women SHG members to establish new enterprises or expand their existing enterprises by availing institutional credit at affordable rates.

During the Financial Year 2025–26, the total turnover from the marketing of products manufactured by women Self Help Groups under DAY-NRLM through Saras Aajeevika Stores and e-commerce platforms is as under:-

1. | Saras Aajeevika Stores | Rs. 2,09,69,691

summaryText:
### OVERVIEW
The Ministry of Rural Development has reported significant progress under the Deendayal Antyodaya Yojana–National Rural Livelihoods Mission (DAY-NRLM) as of July 2026, highlighting the formation of 92 lakh Self Help Groups (SHGs) that have empowered 10.05 crore rural women. The initiative focuses on enhancing financial inclusion through literacy programs, access to institutional credit, and micro-enterprise development, with notable achievements in training and enterprise support during the Financial Year 2025–26.  

### KEY HIGHLIGHTS  
- **92 lakh SHGs** formed across the country, covering **10.05 crore rural women**.  
- **3.81 crore women SHG members** received financial literacy training during FY 2025–26, covering topics such as savings, credit, insurance, and enterprise financing.  
- **Public Sector Banks** and one private bank launched dedicated credit products for women SHG members to support the Lakhpati Didi initiative.  
- **Turnover from women SHG products**:  
  - **Saras Aajeevika Stores**: Rs. 2,09,69,691  
  - **E-commerce platforms**: Rs. 84,49,730.

headline: EMPLOYMENT GENERATION UNDER VB-G RAM G SCHEME
fullText:
Ministry of Rural Development

EMPLOYMENT GENERATION UNDER VB-G RAM G SCHEME

24 JUL 2026 5:41PM by PIB Delhi

The Government continuously reviews the implementation of the programme in consultation with the States/UTs and takes appropriate measures to address implementation-related issues. Under Mahatma Gandhi NREGS, employment was provided to all rural households that demanded work, in accordance with the provisions of the Act. The number of person-days generated varied across States/UTs and financial years depending upon the demand for employment registered by rural households.

The year-wise and States/Union Territories-wise details of persondays generated under Mahatma Gandhi NREGS during the last five financial years is given at Annexure.

For Viksit Bharat – Guarantee for Rozgar and Ajeevika Mission (Gramin) (VB–G RAM G) Act, which has been operationalized with effect from 01.07.2026 a comprehensive online Management Information System (MIS) has been put in place for real time monitoring of implementation and addressing the issues if any faced by implementing agencies. Further, Ministry has deputed large number of officers to the States to review its implementation and provide handholding support to States.

To ensure timely wage payments, the Ministry has implemented the National Electronic Fund Management System (NeFMS) and Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT), under which wages are directly credited into the bank accounts of beneficiaries. The Ministry has also issued detailed Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) prescribing timelines and responsibilities at various levels for timely processing of wage payments. Further, technology-enabled Management Information Systems (MIS) are used for real-time monitoring and tracking of wage payments, pendency at different stages, and regular review with the States/UTs to facilitate timely resolution of issues. In addition, the Ministry regularly releases funds to the States/UTs based on the actual demand from ground and fulfilment of statutory and financial requirements, to ensure smooth implementation of the programme.

The Government has enacted the Viksit Bharat – Guarantee for Rozgar and Ajeevika Mission (Gramin): VB–G RAM G Act, 2025, which has come into force with effect from 1st July, 2026 across all rural areas of the country. The Act strengthens the rural employment guarantee framework by enhancing the statutory employment guarantee from 100 days to 125 days per rural household in a financial year.

The Government has made adequate budgetary provision for effective implementation of the programme. For the financial year 2026–27, a Central share of ₹95,692.31 crore has been provided for the Scheme, representing the highest-ever Budget Estimate for rural employment. With the corresponding estimated State share, the total programme outlay is expected to exceed ₹1.51 lakh crore.

To ensure timely wage disbursal, the Ministry has implemented the National Electronic Fund Management System (NeFMS) and Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT), and established technology-enabled Management Information Systems (MIS) for real-time monitoring and review of wage payments.

The Act accords high priority to Livelihood-related Infrastructure as one of its core thematic areas and adopts a Whole-of-Government Approach through convergence with agriculture, allied sectors and other Government programmes. It permits the creation of rural infrastructure such as Skill Development Centres, Rural Libraries, Digital Learning Centres and other community assets that promote skilling, learning and sustainable livelihoods.

This information was given by Minister of State for the Rural Development, Shri Kamlesh Paswan in a written reply in Rajya Sabha today.

summaryText:
### OVERVIEW  
The Ministry of Rural Development released state-wise data on fund allocations and releases under the Viksit Bharat–Guarantee for Rozgar and Ajeevika Mission (Gramin) (VB-G RAM-G) and the Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Scheme (Mahatma Gandhi NREGS) for the period FY 2021-22 to FY 2025-26, as provided by Minister of State Shri Kamlesh Paswan in a written reply to Rajya Sabha on 24.07.2026.  

### KEY HIGHLIGHTS  
- **VB-G RAM-G Allocations (as of 16.07.2026):** Total interim allocation of ₹93,841.69 crore, with ₹25,495.32 crore released as the first installment across states/UTs. Additional funds of ₹1,850.62 crore allocated for Social Audit and Central Administration.  
- **Mahatma Gandhi NREGS Fund Releases (FY 2021-22 to FY 2025-26):** Total funds released ranged from ₹86,896.47 crore (FY 2025-26) to ₹97,878.01 crore (FY 2021-22).  
- **West Bengal Exception:** Funds were halted on 09.03.2022 due to non-compliance but resumed after state clarifications. No funds were released during FY 2022-23, FY 2023-24, FY 2024-25, or FY 2025-26.  
- **State-wise Employment and Persondays Data:** Annexures III and IV provide state/UT-specific employment numbers and persondays generated under Mahatma Gandhi NREGS over the past five financial years.  
- **Data Source:** Information provided in response to Rajya Sabha Unstarred Question No. 782 dated 24.07.2026.
